summary: Defines the identifiers used in 3GPP systems, including the International Mobile station Equipment Identity (IMEI): an 8-digit Type Allocation Code, a 6-digit serial number, and a check digit. The check digit is computed over the 14 leading digits using the Luhn formula as defined in Annex B of ISO/IEC 7812, and exists to catch manual transmission errors such as mistyped IMEIs at operator customer-care desks.
title: Numbering, addressing and identification
